title: How Republicans Gained an Edge on the Midterm House Map Over 10 Days
summary: Republicans are charging ahead in the nation’s redistricting race, and showing new bullishness after months of growing midterm fears.

title: Two Court Decisions Have Unleashed an Era of Perpetual Redistricting
summary: Four states are considering drawing new maps in the coming weeks. Another dozen or more could join the fray next year.

title: How Minority Districts Fueled the G.O.P.’s Southern Ascendancy in Congress
summary: The “majority-minority” House districts struck down by the Supreme Court last week sent a surge of Black and Hispanic lawmakers to Congress. They also opened opportunities for the G.O.P.

title: Republicans Are Building an Advantage in Redistricting. How Much Is It Worth?
summary: Where things stands in the race for House control after recent court rulings.

title: Deadly Israeli Strikes Erode Cease-Fire in Lebanon
summary: Fighting between Israel and Hezbollah has shown little sign of abating, with Hezbollah also continuing to attack Israeli troops, and both sides accusing the other of violating a U.S.-brokered truce.

title: Long Overlooked, Caspian Sea Provides Strategic Trade Route for Iran
summary: The landlocked body of water has taken on new significance, with Russia shipping military and commercial goods to bolster Tehran’s ability to withstand the U.S. assault.

title: How China’s Leader Lost Faith in His Generals
summary: Xi Jinping spent 13 years building a military to rival that of the United States. But the stronger the Chinese forces grew, the less he trusted the generals he had handpicked to run them.

title: How China May Have Sought to Recruit a House Aide to Spy on the U.S.
summary: A staff member on the House China Committee was promised $10,000 for U.S. policy insights, on issues like Venezuela and rare-earth minerals.

title: Why the Trump-Xi Summit Matters, Even if Little Seems to Come of It
summary: The war in Iran, trade, artificial intelligence and Taiwan are expected to be on the agenda. But expectations are modest.

title: China’s Exports and Imports Set Records in April Amid High Energy Costs
summary: The country’s exports surged and its trade surplus with the United States widened ahead of President Trump’s scheduled visit next week to Beijing.

title: How ‘The View’ Landed at the Center of a Free Speech Battle
summary: The Trump administration’s focus on the show is testament to the enduring influence of an old-fashioned broadcast TV program started 29 years ago.

title: Which Trump Tariffs Are in Place, in the Works or Ruled Illegal
summary: The president has reworked his tariffs repeatedly — sometimes because they have been declared illegal — with more updates still to come.

title: My Ted Talk
summary: Captain Courageous cut a cool swath, in contrast to today’s creepy oligarchs.

title: Forget Climate Change. Democrats Need to Talk About Other Issues.
summary: Climate change is mainly a priority for voters already firmly in Democrats’ camp.

title: Early Adopter
summary: Why does it feel worse to be early than to rush and stress and arrive a little late?

title: Frontier Jet Hits Person on Runway During Takeoff at Denver Airport
summary: The plane reported an engine fire and aborted its takeoff. The people on board were safely evacuated.

title: All Those A.I. Note Takers? They’re Making Lawyers Very Nervous.
summary: A trendy productivity hack, A.I. note takers are capturing every joke and offhand comment in many meetings. They could also potentially waive attorney-client privilege.

title: Britain’s Electorate Is ‘Splintering.’ Can Its System Stand the Strain?
summary: Insurgent parties like Reform U.K. surged at the polls this week. But the British electoral system wasn’t built for multiparty democracy.
